title: Verify server hostnames in SSL/TLS
description: Failing to verify server hostnames during SSL/TLS connections allows man-in-the-middle attacks where attackers can present valid certificates for different hostnames. Hostname verification ensures the certificate matches the intended server.
recommendation: Enable hostname verification in SSL/TLS connections and ensure certificates match the target hostname.

noncompliant.description: Disabling hostname verification or using incorrect hostnames allows man-in-the-middle attacks with valid certificates for different hosts.

compliant.description: Enabling and properly configuring hostname verification ensures that TLS certificates match the intended server hostname.

checks.message: Server hostnames should be verified in SSL/TLS connections to prevent man-in-the-middle attacks.
